blocks.body: Define your components in TypeScript using `@storyblok/schema`, push them to Storyblok with the Management API, and fetch content with full type safety using `@storyblok/api-client`.
blocks.headline: Schema-first Storyblok development

blocks.text:
### Why schema-first?

Defining your Storyblok components in TypeScript means your content model and your application code stay in sync. Rename a field in your schema and the compiler tells you every place that needs updating.

No more runtime surprises — just confidence from editor to production.
blocks.caption: The monoblok monorepo — schema-first Storyblok development.

items.title: Type-safe schema
items.description: Define reusable fields and components in TypeScript. Types flow from schema to fetch calls.

items.title: Management API client
items.description: Push components and stories to Storyblok programmatically using @storyblok/management-api-client.

items.title: Content Delivery API client
items.description: Fetch stories with a typed client that understands your schema's discriminated unions.

content.seo_description: A demo site built with Storyblok, @storyblok/schema, and Qwik.
